terms.description: Curated terms that belong in a back-of-book index and glossary. Each entry is a concept worth looking up — not a fragment, not a generic word, not the topic the entire book is about.

term.description: Canonical display form of the term, properly cased. Example: "OAuth 2.0", not "oauth 2.0" or "OAUTH 2.0".

definition.description: One-sentence glossary definition. Should be understandable without reading the source material. No more than ~30 words.

parent.description: The `term` value of a broader concept this term belongs under. Creates hierarchy in the index. Example: "OAuth" might have parent "authentication". Omit if the term is top-level.

aliases.description: Other surface forms that refer to the same concept. Used to merge candidate variants. Must be exact candidate term strings from the input so post-processing can map locations. Example: ["OAuth 2.0", "OAuth2"] for canonical term "OAuth".

see_also.description: Related terms the reader might also want to look up. Each value must be another term's `term` field in this output.

main_files.description: Files where this term is substantively discussed (not just mentioned). These become bold page numbers in the index. Values are filenames from the candidate locations.

suggested.description: Important terms that the automated extraction missed but that clearly belong in the index based on the source material context. These have no candidate locations — they will be located by searching the source files in post-processing.

parent.description: Parent term for hierarchy (omit if top-level).
